landing-page.description: Plan, design, build, and verify a landing page.

workflow.validation: run the project's narrowest build/test or page check
workflow.recovery: if scope is unclear, emit the agent list and ask the missing context questions before edits

questions: What framework is the project using?
What is the offer, audience, and primary CTA?
Is this a new page or an edit to an existing page?

steps.reason: Sharpen the offer, audience, and CTA before designing.
Set visual hierarchy, typography, layout, and interaction quality.
Catch semantic, contrast, and keyboard issues during design.
Build the smallest correct implementation with existing patterns.
Apply the final product-quality pass.
Verify performance, responsiveness, and accessibility at runtime.

pr-review.description: Review a pull request for bugs, regressions, security, and missing tests.

workflow.output: findings ordered by severity with file references
workflow.validation: use git diff, tests, or static checks as available
workflow.recovery: if no PR is available, ask for branch, diff, or PR URL

questions: What branch or PR should be reviewed?
Should the review include security and performance checks?

steps.reason: Review Standards and Spec independently.
Check trust boundaries, secrets, injection, and auth gaps.
Check that changed behavior has regression coverage.

bug-fix.description: Reproduce, isolate, fix, and verify a bug.

workflow.output: fix with reproduction and verification evidence
workflow.validation: run the failing command or narrow regression test
workflow.recovery: if reproduction is missing, gather exact error and command first

questions: What is the exact failure or error?
What command or user path reproduces it?

steps.reason: Build a tight feedback loop before theorizing.
Patch the smallest shared source of the bug.
Lock the bug down with a red-green regression test.
Review the fix for regressions and scope.

fullstack-feature.description: Build a user-facing feature across UI, API, data, and tests.

workflow.validation: run focused tests or build for touched surface
workflow.recovery: if contracts are unclear, stop after documenting the needed API/data decision

questions: What is the user-visible success criteria?
What existing APIs, routes, or data models should be reused?

steps.reason: Resolve the decision tree before building.
Reuse existing patterns and choose the smallest correct slice.
Design the UI structure when the task has a UI surface.
Drive each behavior through a red-green slice.
Apply React conventions when React is explicit.
Apply the product-quality gate.
Review Standards and Spec before committing.

video-campaign.description: Create or repair a professional video campaign with motion design, scripts, brand consistency, platform SEO, and render verification.

phases: inventory source assets and target platform constraints
lock brand system, script hooks, and SEO keyword map
build motion compositions and render pilots
batch render and verify campaign manifest
workflow.output: rendered video campaign package with scripts, metadata, Missinglettr copy, and verification evidence
workflow.validation: probe rendered media durations, inspect sample frames, verify manifests and platform metadata
workflow.recovery: if video runtime is unavailable, use the narrowest local renderer and keep source compositions portable

questions: What assets, target keywords, and platform formats should be used?
Should this produce final renders or a storyboard/pilot first?

steps.reason: Select the right video workflow and composition contract.
Set visual identity, logo, color, typography, and motion consistency.
Write platform-aware hooks, voiceover scripts, captions, and CTAs.
Map keywords into titles, descriptions, tags, and Shorts metadata.
Apply professional animation, transitions, kinetic text, and visual rhythm.
Render and validate platform-ready video outputs.
Final product-quality pass before handoff.

research.description: Research a topic, competitor, technology, or market and synthesize recommendations.

workflow.validation: cite concrete sources or repo evidence
workflow.recovery: if sources are unavailable, mark claims as unverified

questions: What decision should the research support?
Are web sources required or should this be repo-only?

steps.reason: Define the decision the research must support.
Stop at the smallest evidence-backed recommendation.

workflow.validation: rerun the same benchmark or focused timing check
workflow.recovery: if no metric exists, establish baseline before changing code

questions: What metric is too slow?
What command, page, or endpoint should be measured?

steps.reason: Establish a baseline before changing code.
Patch the smallest measured bottleneck.
Repeat the same measurement for before-after proof.

security-review.description: Review code or architecture for security risk.

workflow.output: security findings with impact and remediation
workflow.validation: prove findings with code references or commands
workflow.recovery: if boundary is unclear, ask for the asset and attacker model

questions: What trust boundary or asset matters most?
Should this be a review only or include fixes?

steps.reason: Map the trust boundary and attacker model.
Trace the control flow methodically.
Prove findings through code references or a focused test.

agent-cleanup.description: Clean, rename, validate, and reorganize an agent collection.

workflow.recovery: preserve raw source files unless deletion is explicit

questions: What agents should be public versus archived?
What host tool should the agents target first?

steps.reason: Inventory agent roles, overlaps, and host constraints.
Delete duplication and keep the smallest useful agent surface.
Review registry and agent-definition consistency.

api-test.description: Validate API contracts, behavior, security, and failure handling.

workflow.output: API test plan or fixes with evidence
workflow.validation: run focused API tests or documented request checks
workflow.recovery: if no contract exists, derive expected behavior from routes and docs

questions: Which endpoint, contract, or webhook should be tested?
Should this create tests or only review behavior?

steps.reason: Identify the contract and observable behavior.
Check trust-boundary and failure-path behavior.
Exercise happy paths and failure paths through the contract.

workflow.output: clear docs grounded in current repo behavior
workflow.validation: run or verify every documented command where practical
workflow.recovery: if behavior cannot be verified, mark it explicitly

questions: Who is the doc for?
Should commands be verified or documented from source only?

steps.reason: Ground the document in the smallest current source of truth.
Review commands, claims, and scope against source.

deployment.description: Prepare, debug, or review deployment and release flow.

workflow.validation: run the narrowest build, CI, or deploy dry-run command
workflow.recovery: if credentials are missing, stop at config validation and document the blocker

questions: What host or CI system is used?
Is this a deploy fix, release checklist, or environment setup?

steps.reason: Inspect the current deployment path and reproduce the blocker.
Make the smallest deployment or configuration change.
Run the narrowest build, CI, or dry-run check.

triage.description: Classify an unknown task and choose the smallest useful agent set.

workflow.recovery: if confidence is low, show alternates instead of pretending certainty

questions: What are you trying to produce or fix?
What project path should be inspected?

steps.reason: Ask the smallest question that classifies the task.

improve-plan.description: Create a cost-aware execution plan before implementation.

workflow.output: short execution plan with agent/model routing and credit controls
workflow.validation: plan names concrete files/modules, commands, and what not to spawn
workflow.recovery: if scope is unclear, stop at questions and do not implement

questions: What outcome should the plan optimize for?
What files, modules, or repo path should be inspected before planning?

steps.reason: Resolve load-bearing decisions before implementation.
Use the cheapest sufficient execution shape.

ui-ux-review.description: Review or plan UI/UX with design-system, accessibility, responsive layout, and polish checks.

workflow.output: UI/UX plan or review with concrete fixes and verification checks
workflow.validation: inspect small and large viewports, keyboard/touch states, contrast, reduced motion, and layout stability
workflow.recovery: if the target UI is unclear, ask for page/component path and screenshots before edits

questions: What page, component, or flow should be reviewed?
Is this a design plan, implementation, or polish pass?

steps.reason: Review design-system, layout, and interaction quality.
Review contrast, input, motion, and assistive states.
Verify responsive and runtime behavior.

impeccable-polish.description: Final product-quality pass for professional fit, finish, consistency, and obvious rough edges.

workflow.output: polish findings or fixes with proof
workflow.validation: run focused UI/runtime checks and document before/after evidence
workflow.recovery: if no target is provided, produce a prioritized polish checklist instead of editing

questions: What screen, workflow, or artifact needs the polish pass?
Should this only review, or also apply fixes?

steps.reason: Find visible hierarchy, spacing, and interaction rough edges.
Apply the smallest product-quality fixes.
Verify responsive, performance, and accessibility behavior.

governance-gateway.description: Design or review an execution governance gateway for agent actions.

workflow.output: governance contract or review with decision evidence
workflow.validation: prove state-changing actions are blocked without mandate, evidence, scope, and named authority
workflow.recovery: if authority, scope, or evidence is missing, deny or require named human approval

questions: What actions need gateway enforcement?
Which tools, credentials, or resources must be withheld from agents?

steps.reason: Map authority, evidence, scope, and trust boundaries.
Define the smallest enforceable governance contract.
Prove unauthorized state changes are denied.
